source: Learning bash with Rocky - Appendix: Variables With Logs: Using variables to extract dated entries from a log and email them

questions.q: Why does the log-extraction script need special handling for dnf.log?
options: dnf.log and messages format the date differently, so the same date approach will not work for both
dnf.log is owned by a different user
dnf.log uses a different character encoding
questions.why: dnf.log uses a yyyy-mm-dd style while /var/log/messages uses "May  4"; the script must match dnf.log's format.
+%F gives the ISO yyyy-mm-dd form, matching the dnf.log date.

questions.q: Why store today=$(date +%F) in a variable?
options: To hold today's date in the dnf.log format so it can be grepped from the log
questions.why: The variable is then used to search the log for today's entries.

questions.q: How does the script pull today's lines out of the log?
questions.why: grep matches the date variable against the log file.

questions.q: How does the script send the results, and how is it run daily?
options: Pipe grep's output to mail -s "subject" address; automate it with a cron job
print it and read it manually
questions.why: The output is piped to /bin/mail; a cron job runs the script daily.
